name: Kingston Pregnancy Care Centre
description: Support for unexpected pregnancies, including baby clothes and parenting classes.

name: Hospice Kingston (Providence Care)
hours_text: Residence: 24/7. Community Programs: Mon-Fri 8:30 AM – 4:30 PM.
application_process: Referral required for residence. Self-referral for some community programs.
description: Provides compassionate end-of-life care and bereavement support. Now integrated with Providence Care, operating the AB Smith Homestead House residence.

name: Alzheimer Society KFL&A
description: Support, education, and advocacy for people with dementia and their caregivers.

name: Stroke Network of Southeastern Ontario
address: 76 Stuart St, Watkins 3, Rm 4-3-409-0, Kingston, ON K7L 2V7
description: Administrative body facilitating best practices for stroke care, provider education, and survivor support resources. Does not provide direct emergency medical treatment.

name: Autism Ontario (East Region)
description: Information and support for families and individuals on the autism spectrum.

name: CNIB Foundation Kingston
description: Community support, rehab, and advocacy for people with sight loss.

name: March of Dimes Canada - Employment Services
address: 920 Princess St, Suite 360, Kingston, ON K7L 1H1
description: Specializes in vocational rehabilitation and employment support for people with disabilities, offering assistive technology assessments and job coaching.

name: AMS Food Bank (Queen's)
address: 87 Union Street, Room 212, Kingston, ON K7L 2N9
hours_text: Mon & Wed: 5:00 PM – 7:00 PM. Thu: 12:00 PM – 2:00 PM. (Fall 2025). PEACH Market: Mon/Wed/Fri 11am-2pm.
description: Confidential and non-judgmental food service tailored specifically for the Queen's University community. Provides non-perishable food, personal care items, and facilitates the Good Food Box program.

name: SLC Food Pantry
application_process: Online intake form to book collection time.
description: Emergency food pantry providing non-perishable food items and personal care products for St. Lawrence College students.

name: Community Harvest Kingston
hours_text: Office: Mon-Fri 8:30 AM – 4:30 PM. Good Food Box Pickup: 3rd Thursday of month.
fees: Good Food Box is for purchase (cost-recovery).
description: Initiative focused on long-term food security through fresh produce distribution (Good Food Box) and community gardening support.

name: Memorial Centre Farmers Market
description: 100% producer-run market selling food and goods grown or produced within the Kingston foodshed (100km radius).

name: Queen's Legal Aid
address: 303 Bagot Street, Suite 500, Kingston, ON K7K 5W7
hours_text: Mon-Thu: 8:30 AM – 4:30 PM. Fri: 1:00 PM – 4:30 PM.
description: Provides legal services to low-income residents and students, handling summary conviction offences, tenant disputes, and tribunal hearings.

name: KFL&A Public Health - Immunization
hours_text: Routine Clinics: Tue (All Ages), Wed (0-18). By Appointment Only. General: M-F 8:30-4:30.
description: Provides publicly funded vaccines, including routine childhood immunizations and influenza vaccines.

name: KFL&A Public Health: Sexual Health Clinic
hours_text: Quick Test Clinic (Walk-in): Tue & Thu 1:00 PM – 3:30 PM. Appointments: Mon-Fri 8:30 AM – 4:30 PM.
application_process: Walk-in for Quick Test; Call for other appointments.
documents_required: Health card not required for Quick Test.
fees: Most services free; some contraceptives at cost.
description: Provides confidential sexual health services including STI testing, birth control, and emergency contraception.

name: Sexual Assault Centre Kingston (SACK)
address: 400 Elliott Avenue, Unit 1, Kingston, ON K7K 6M9
hours_text: Crisis Phone: 24/7. Chat/Text: Daily 12:00 PM – Midnight. Office: Mon-Fri 9:00 AM – 4:00 PM.
application_process: Call crisis line or office for intake.
description: Provides free, confidential support for survivors of sexual violence. Services include 24/7 crisis line, counselling, and accompaniment to medical/legal appointments.

name: Kingston General Hospital (KGH) Emergency Department
address: 41 King Street West, Kingston, ON K7L 2V7
hours_text: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week.
fees: Covered by OHIP. Fees for non-residents/uninsured.
description: A full-service Level 1 Trauma Centre providing 24/7 care for life-threatening injuries and severe illnesses.

name: Hotel Dieu Hospital Urgent Care Centre (KHSC)
hours_text: Daily: 8:00 AM – 8:00 PM (Registration may close early if capacity is reached).
fees: Covered by OHIP. Fees apply for non-residents.
description: Provides assessment and treatment for non-life-threatening injuries and illnesses (e.g., cuts, sprains, infections) that cannot wait for a family doctor.

name: Providence Care Hospital
address: 752 King St W, Kingston, ON K7L 4X3
hours_text: Inpatient: 24/7. Outpatient clinics: Mon-Fri Business Hours.
description: Region's leading facility for aging, mental health, and rehabilitative care. Integrates long-term mental health care, physical rehabilitation, and complex continuing care.

name: Kingston Military Family Resource Centre (KMFRC)
description: Provides support services, childcare, and mental health resources for military families.

name: TransFamily Kingston
description: Grassroots organization providing peer support, social gatherings, and advocacy for transgender and gender-diverse individuals and their families.

name: KFL&A Children's Services
description: Child care subsidy and special needs resourcing.

name: Rural Frontenac Community Services
address: 1109 Garrett St, Sharbot Lake, ON K0H 2P0
description: Multi-service agency for rural townships. Services include transportation, youth programs, seniors' supports (Meals on Wheels), and child care.

name: Southern Frontenac Community Services
description: Provides senior support (Adult Day Programs, Diners Club), food security (Food Bank, Meals on Wheels), and transportation services for South Frontenac.

name: Kingston East Community Centre
description: Modern community hub with gymnasium, fitness centre, and community kitchen.

name: EarlyON Child and Family Centre
description: Provides free, high-quality drop-in programs for families with children aged 0-6, focusing on early learning and development.

name: BGC South East (Boys & Girls Club)
hours_text: Admin: Mon-Fri 8:30 AM – 4:30 PM. Programs: 3:00 PM – 6:00 PM.
description: Offers extensive recreational, educational, and life-skills programming for children and youth, including camps and aquatic programs.

name: YMCA of Eastern Ontario - Kingston
hours_text: Mon-Thu: 5:30 AM – 9:00 PM. Fri: 5:30 AM – 7:00 PM. Sat-Sun: 7:00 AM – 4:00 PM.
description: Provides fitness, recreation, and childcare services.

name: Kingston Humane Society
description: Animal shelter offering pet adoption and emergency pet food assistance.

name: St. John Ambulance
description: First aid training and community medical service volunteers.

name: Canadian Red Cross - Kingston
description: Disaster relief, senior transportation, and meals on wheels.

name: United Way KFL&A
description: Fundraising and grant-making organization coordinating regional initiatives like the Backpack Program. Primarily funds other agencies rather than direct service delivery.

name: Community Foundation for Kingston & Area
address: 275 Ontario St, Suite 100, Kingston, ON K7K 2X5
description: Philanthropic organization focused on grant-making to registered charities. Manages funds like the Regina Rosen Food First Fund.

name: Habitat for Humanity Kingston
description: Affordable homeownership program and ReStore.

name: Kingston Literacy & Skills
address: 16 Bath Rd, Suite 104, Kingston, ON K7L 1C4
description: Provides adult literacy training, LBS upgrading, and LINC (Language Instruction for Newcomers to Canada) programs.

name: KEYS Job Centre
hours_text: Mon-Fri: 8:30 AM – 4:30 PM. (Tue open to 6:00 PM).
description: Employment and newcomer services offering job search assistance, workshops, and settlement support.

name: ACFOMI
hours_text: Mon: 8:30 AM – 6:00 PM. Tue-Thu: 8:30 AM – 4:30 PM. Fri: 8:30 AM – 3:00 PM.
description: Provides employment and settlement services for French-speaking newcomers and residents.

name: ReStart Employment Services
hours_text: Mon-Thu: 8:30 AM – 4:30 PM. Fri: 8:30 AM – 4:00 PM.
description: Offers employment services and housing support to help individuals find work and maintain housing.

name: St. Lawrence College Employment Service
description: Provides comprehensive job search support for both students and the general public, leveraging the college's resources for career development.

name: City of Kingston - Housing and Social Services (Ontario Works)
documents_required: Proof of income, assets, shelter costs, ID.
description: Provides financial and employment assistance to people in financial need.

name: ODSP Kingston Office
description: Ontario Disability Support Program financial assistance.

name: Service Canada Centre
description: Federal government services including EI and CPP.

name: ServiceOntario
description: Provincial government services including health cards and driving licenses.

name: Kingston Police (Non-Emergency)
description: Police services for reporting non-emergency crimes or concerns.

name: OPP Frontenac Detachment
description: Provincial police services for the surrounding county areas.

name: COAST (Crisis Outreach)
description: Mobile crisis intervention team partnering mental health workers with police.

name: Maltby Centre
address: 31 Hyperion Court, Suite 100, Kingston, ON K7K 7G3
fees: Mental health is free. Some autism services fee-for-service.
description: Provides mental health and autism services for children and youth.

name: Pathways for Children and Youth (Merged)
description: MERGED: Pathways for Children and Youth has formally merged with the Maltby Centre. Please refer to Maltby Centre for all child and youth mental health services.

name: Geneva Centre for Autism (Virtual)
description: Online resources and training for families.

name: 211 Ontario
description: A helpline and online database of Ontario's community and social services. 211 helps people find the right community and social services.

name: Black Youth Helpline
description: Serves all youth and specifically responds to the need for a Black youth specific service. Focuses on family, schools, and community.
